TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: izua i Kaɫuyung azua ɫa Paiquluvuy a namasan paɫi, aya a tjautsikel.
S.PHON: izua i kaʎujuŋ azua ʎa paiquɭuvuj a namasan paʎi, aja a cauʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: izua i Kaljuyung azua lja Paiquluvuy a namasan palji, aya a tjaucikel.
S.PHON: izua i kaʎujuŋ azua ʎa paiquɣuvuj a namasan paʎi, aja a ʦauʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: There was a person of the Paiquluvuy family in Kaɫuyung who became an evil-eye man, according to the story.

S.FORM: nu kivangavang azua, izua patsunan a sasiq anema; paridiw a matsay, aya.
S.PHON: nu kivaŋavaŋ azua, izua paʦunan a sasiq anəma; paridiw a maʦaj, aja.
S.FORM: nu kivangavang azua, izua pacunan a sasiq anema; paridiw a macay, aya.
S.PHON: nu kivaŋavaŋ azua, izua paʦunan a sasiq anəma; paRidiw a maʦaj, aja.
S.TRANSL: As he walked about, ants or anything else he saw died, they say.

S.FORM: "aku mayaya sun? nasa uri nakuya sun," aya a ɫamadju.
S.PHON: "aku majaja sunʔ nasa uri nakuja sun," aja a ʎamaɉu.
S.FORM: "aku mayaya sun' nasa uri nakuya sun," aya a ljamadju.
S.PHON: "aku majaja sunʔ nasa uRi nakuja sun," aja a ʎamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: “Why are you like that? You’re going to be someone bad,” people said.

S.FORM: sivaik a sema pana; uri paqayam tua tsiqaw.
S.PHON: sivaik a səma pana; uri paqajam tua ʦiqaw.
S.FORM: sivaik a sema pana; uri paqayam tua ciqaw.
S.PHON: sivaik a səma pana; uRi paqajam tua ʦiqaw.
S.TRANSL: They took him to the river to see the fish;

S.FORM: manu ka kiqenetj tua tsiqaw, matsay anga.
S.PHON: manu ka kiqənəc tua ʦiqaw, maʦaj aŋa.
S.FORM: manu ka kiqenetj tua ciqaw, macay anga.
S.PHON: manu ka kiqənəʦ tua ʦiqaw, maʦaj aŋa.
S.TRANSL: and when he looked at the fish they died.

S.FORM: "ai nasa uri ka paɫi sun," aya zazua salasaladj.
S.PHON: "ai nasa uri ka paʎi sun," aja zazua saɭasaɭaɉ.
S.FORM: "ai nasa uri ka palji sun," aya zazua salasaladj.
S.PHON: "ai nasa uRi ka paʎi sun," aja zazua saɣasaɣaɉ.
S.TRANSL: “Oh! It seems you’re becoming an evil-eye man,” said his companions.

S.FORM: saka tsemikel a sema umaq.
S.PHON: saka ʦəmikəɭ a səma umaq.
S.FORM: saka cemikel a sema umaq.
S.PHON: saka ʦəmikəɣ a səma umaq.
S.TRANSL: So they went back home.

S.FORM: ɫakua sivaik a sema pasa navanavaɫ.
S.PHON: ʎakua sivaik a səma pasa navanavaʎ.
S.FORM: ljakua sivaik a sema pasa navanavalj.
S.PHON: ʎakua sivaik a səma pasa navanavaʎ.
S.TRANSL: But they took him down south,

S.FORM: sa san tapavan.
S.PHON: sa san tapavan.
S.FORM: sa san tapavan.
S.PHON: sa san tapavan.
S.TRANSL: and made him a house.

S.FORM: saka izuanga timadju a matsidiɫ.
S.PHON: saka izuaŋa timaɉu a maʦidiʎ.
S.FORM: saka izuanga timadju a macidilj.
S.PHON: saka izuaŋa timaɉu a maʦidiʎ.
S.TRANSL: And he was there by himself.

S.FORM: "azua nu kiteveɫ tua salasaladj, azua semengats," aya.
S.PHON: "azua nu kitəvəʎ tua saɭasaɭaɉ, azua səməŋaʦ," aja.
S.FORM: "azua nu kitevelj tua salasaladj, azua semengac," aya.
S.PHON: "azua nu kitəvəʎ tua saɣasaɣaɉ, azua səməŋaʦ," aja.
S.TRANSL: “When he is with his former companions he is dangerous,” they said.

S.FORM: avanu sikitjukuda.
S.PHON: avanu sikicukuda.
S.FORM: avanu sikitjukuda.
S.PHON: avanu sikiʦukuda.
S.TRANSL: That’s why he had to live apart.

S.FORM: saka zua paɫi a papatsay timadju, marekutj a maɫaqaɫan a qaliqali.
S.PHON: saka zua paʎi a papaʦaj timaɉu, marəkuc a maʎaqaʎan a qaɭiqaɭi.
S.FORM: saka zua palji a papacay timadju, marekutj a maljaqaljan a qaliqali.
S.PHON: saka zua paʎi a papaʦaj timaɉu, maRəkuʦ a maʎaqaʎan a qaɣiqaɣi.
S.TRANSL: So because the evil-eye man could kill, people of other villages were afraid.

S.FORM: saka avanu sipakitjaviliɫ tua vava tua vutjuɫ tua qavay tua kinsa, aya.
S.PHON: saka avanu sipakicaviɭiʎ tua vava tua vucuʎ tua qavaj tua kinsa, aja.
S.FORM: saka avanu sipakitjavililj tua vava tua vutjulj tua qavay tua kinsa, aya.
S.PHON: saka avanu sipakiʦaviɣiʎ tua vava tua vuʦuʎ tua qavaj tua kinsa, aja.
S.TRANSL: So they took turns to give him beer, meat, dumplings and other food, they say.

S.FORM: manu nasa masupil anga azua maɫaqaɫan, sa sunasii nua se Vungaliz, sa ɫuɫu-i.
S.PHON: manu nasa masupiɭ aŋa azua maʎaqaʎan, sa sunasii nua sə vuŋaɭiz, sa ʎuʎu-i.
S.FORM: manu nasa masupil anga azua maljaqaljan, sa sunasii nua se Vungaliz, sa ljulju-i.
S.PHON: manu nasa masupiɣ aŋa azua maʎaqaʎan, sa sunasii nua sə vuŋaɣiz, sa ʎuʎu-i.
S.TRANSL: But it seems the people got tired of it, and the people of Vungaliz killed him and picked up (his head);

S.FORM: manu sivaik azua tjaɫuzua i pana i Tjuakalangan.
S.PHON: manu sivaik azua caʎuzua i pana i cuakaɭaŋan.
S.FORM: manu sivaik azua tjaljuzua i pana i Tjuakalangan.
S.PHON: manu sivaik azua ʦaʎuzua i pana i ʦuakaɣaŋan.
S.TRANSL: and took it to the river at Tjuakalangan.

S.FORM: "tja qayamaw," aya zazua.
S.PHON: "ca qajamaw," aja zazua.
S.FORM: "tja qayamaw," aya zazua.
S.PHON: "ʦa qajamaw," aja zazua.
S.TRANSL: “Let’s have a look,” they said.

S.FORM: manu ka suqulengan a mudingan, manu matsay a se Vungaliz.
S.PHON: manu ka suquɭəŋan a mudiŋan, manu maʦaj a sə vuŋaɭiz.
S.FORM: manu ka suqulengan a mudingan, manu macay a se Vungaliz.
S.PHON: manu ka suquɣəŋan a mudiŋan, manu maʦaj a sə vuŋaɣiz.
S.TRANSL: But when they uncovered the face, the Vungaliz people died.

S.FORM: saka matsidiɫ anga a pinatseqisan a pi tsungal, aya.
S.PHON: saka maʦidiʎ aŋa a pinaʦəqisan a pi ʦuŋaɭ, aja.
S.FORM: saka macidilj anga a pinaceqisan a pi cungal, aya.
S.PHON: saka maʦidiʎ aŋa a pinaʦəqisan a pi ʦuŋaɣ, aja.
S.TRANSL: One survived wounded in the knee from his glare, they say.

S.FORM: saka matsidiɫ anga nu pakaqiaw sema umaq i Vungaliz.
S.PHON: saka maʦidiʎ aŋa nu pakaqiaw səma umaq i vuŋaɭiz.
S.FORM: saka macidilj anga nu pakaqiaw sema umaq i Vungaliz.
S.PHON: saka maʦidiʎ aŋa nu pakaqiaw səma umaq i vuŋaɣiz.
S.TRANSL: He went off alone back home to Vungaliz calling for help.

S.FORM: "matsay amen," aya, aya a tjautsikel.
S.PHON: "maʦaj amən," aja, aja a cauʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: "macay amen," aya, aya a tjaucikel.
S.PHON: "maʦaj amən," aja, aja a ʦauʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: “We’re being killed,” he said, according to the story.
